report_metadata.step_name: Match Assets to Site Plan Drawing Symbols
report_metadata.step_description: Using the asset records from Step 7, the text annotations extracted from the site plan in Step 5 (sub-step-extract-all-notes), and the drawing legend from Step 6, locate each asset in the site plan drawing text and identify which legend symbol is associated with it. For each asset record: (1) Strip all leading alphabetic prefix characters and any leading zeros from the asset_id to produce a bare numeric ID (e.g. SLGT00302876 → 302876, CB00045 → 45, TX0001234 → 1234). (2) Search the Step 5 extracted site plan text for the bare numeric ID as a text token or label — asset IDs appear as numeric labels adjacent to their drawing symbols in the annotated text. (3) When found, examine the text context immediately surrounding the bare ID to infer which drawing symbol is associated with it, then match that context to the best-fitting legend entry from Step 6 (comparing symbol descriptions and labels). Set symbol_description and label from the matched legend entry and match_status to 'found'. (4) If the bare ID is not found in the extracted text, set symbol_description and label to null and match_status to 'not_found'. Output an updated_assets array containing every original asset record from Step 7 with four new fields added: bare_id, symbol_description, label, and match_status. Also output asset_symbol_matches (the raw per-asset match results) and match_summary totals.
report_metadata.validation_criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'updated_assets' (array), 'asset_symbol_matches' (array), and 'match_summary' (object). 'updated_assets' must contain every original asset record from Step 7 with four additional fields: 'bare_id' (numeric-only string, no prefix letters, no leading zeros), 'symbol_description' (string or null), 'label' (string or null), and 'match_status' (exactly 'found' or 'not_found'). All original TAL fields must be preserved. 'asset_symbol_matches' must contain one entry per asset with 'asset_id', 'bare_id', 'symbol_description', 'label', and 'match_status'. 'match_summary' must include 'total', 'found', and 'not_found' integer counts that sum to total. A not_found count equal to total is acceptable when no asset IDs appear in the extracted site plan text — this is not a failure. Entries with match_status 'found' must have non-null symbol_description and label.

files_read.description: Output file produced by the agent

files_output.description: Validation test report generated by step validator

execution_context.prompt:
## STEP NAME
Match Assets to Site Plan Drawing Symbols

## STEP DESCRIPTION
Using the asset records from Step 7, the text annotations extracted from the site plan in Step 5 (sub-step-extract-all-notes), and the drawing legend from Step 6, locate each asset in the site plan drawing text and identify which legend symbol is associated with it. For each asset record: (1) Strip all leading alphabetic prefix characters and any leading zeros from the asset_id to produce a bare numeric ID (e.g. SLGT00302876 → 302876, CB00045 → 45, TX0001234 → 1234). (2) Search the Step 5 extracted site plan text for the bare numeric ID as a text token or label — asset IDs appear as numeric labels adjacent to their drawing symbols in the annotated text. (3) When found, examine the text context immediately surrounding the bare ID to infer which drawing symbol is associated with it, then match that context to the best-fitting legend entry from Step 6 (comparing symbol descriptions and labels). Set symbol_description and label from the matched legend entry and match_status to 'found'. (4) If the bare ID is not found in the extracted text, set symbol_description and label to null and match_status to 'not_found'. Output an updated_assets array containing every original asset record from Step 7 with four new fields added: bare_id, symbol_description, label, and match_status. Also output asset_symbol_matches (the raw per-asset match results) and match_summary totals.

## INPUT DATA (metadata/context sent to agent)
{"asset_count": 18, "legend_count": 24}

## VALIDATION CRITERIA
Must return a JSON object with 'updated_assets' (array), 'asset_symbol_matches' (array), and 'match_summary' (object). 'updated_assets' must contain every original asset record from Step 7 with four additional fields: 'bare_id' (numeric-only string, no prefix letters, no leading zeros), 'symbol_description' (string or null), 'label' (string or null), and 'match_status' (exactly 'found' or 'not_found'). All original TAL fields must be preserved. 'asset_symbol_matches' must contain one entry per asset with 'asset_id', 'bare_id', 'symbol_description', 'label', and 'match_status'. 'match_summary' must include 'total', 'found', and 'not_found' integer counts that sum to total. A not_found count equal to total is acceptable when no asset IDs appear in the extracted site plan text — this is not a failure. Entries with match_status 'found' must have non-null symbol_description and label.

## YOUR TASK
Generate and execute structured test cases against the agent's output. Use the FULL FILE CONTENTS (when provided) as your primary evidence. Cross-reference every item between input and output files. For each test case, document: the input data examined, the expected output, the actual output found, step-by-step execution notes of how you validated, and your final reasoning. Respond with the JSON test report.
